title: Weak Authentication Bypass
description: Due to the lack of information regarding the authentication mechanism, an attacker could potentially bypass authentication controls. This could involve exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication process itself, such as weak password policies, flawed session management, or the absence of multi-factor authentication. Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to impersonate a legitimate user.
attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, credential stuffing, brute-force attacks, or session hijacking.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Regularly review and update authentication protocols and libraries.
